@@ -222,6 +222,27 @@ const buildPools = (classCapacity) => ({
   seniorPwd:   { capacity: SENIOR_PWD_POOL_PER_CLASS, taken: 0 },
 });
 
+// ============================================================================
+// ReservedPoolBadge — small chip used in seat pickers, manifests, and the
+// walk-in daily list to mark seats/passengers belonging to a reserved pool.
+// ============================================================================
+function ReservedPoolBadge({ pool, size = 'sm' }) {
+  if (pool === 'regular' || !pool) return null;
+  const palette = pool === 'govHospital'
+    ? { bg: '#E9D5FF', fg: '#5B21B6', label: 'GOV/HOSPITAL POOL' }
+    : { bg: '#FEF3C7', fg: '#92400E', label: 'SENIOR/PWD POOL' };
+  const padding = size === 'sm' ? 'px-1.5 py-0.5' : 'px-2 py-1';
+  const text = size === 'sm' ? 'text-[9px]' : 'text-[11px]';
+  return (
+    <span
+      className={`inline-block rounded font-bold tracking-wide ${padding} ${text}`}
+      style={{ background: palette.bg, color: palette.fg }}
+    >
+      {palette.label}
+    </span>
+  );
+}
+
 // ============================================================================
 // TIER 1: LANDING PAGE
 // ============================================================================


Investigate per the method in your instructions, then return the findings list.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-29 22:35:14Z)**_

I've read the changed region in full. Analysis:

**File**: `docs/vercel_mockup/src/MOCKUP.jsx` (a static mockup, not production code)

**Change**: Adds a pure presentational React component `ReservedPoolBadge({ pool, size })`.

**Sink analysis**:
- `pool` prop is only used in equality comparisons (`=== 'regular'`, `=== 'govHospital'`); it is never interpolated into JSX, className, style, or any sink.
- `size` prop is only used in an equality check (`=== 'sm'`); never interpolated.
- The rendered text `{palette.label}` is selected from a hardcoded palette object — React auto-escapes; not attacker-controlled.
- `style={{ background: palette.bg, color: palette.fg }}` uses hardcoded hex values from the hardcoded palette.
- `className` template literal interpolates `padding` and `text`, both chosen from hardcoded literal options based on the equality check.

**Entry points / data flow**: None. There are no HTTP handlers, no shells, no SQL, no eval, no dangerouslySetInnerHTML, no URL/path/log sinks. Even if `pool`/`size` were attacker-controlled, neither value reaches any sink — they only gate which hardcoded constants are selected.

**Phase 2c checks**: No observability sinks, no IaC, no CI triggers, no allowlist edits, no permission grants, no identity teardown, no removed validator, no fail-open state, no security registry, no auth gate, no resource cap, no shell/path/loader sink. None applicable.

No security-relevant findings.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-29 22:35:18Z)**_

No findings. The diff adds a pure presentational React badge component in a static mockup file. The two props (`pool`, `size`) are used only in equality comparisons to select between hardcoded palette/className constants — neither prop is interpolated into any sink (text, style, className, URL, log, shell, etc.), and React auto-escapes the hardcoded label text. No entry points or sinks introduced.
